title: Breadcrumb Navigation with Object Selector Dropdown
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want a breadcrumb with an object selector dropdown on the detail page, so that I can see where I am in the application and quickly switch to another Salesforce object without navigating back to the scorecard.
description:
The object detail page needs a breadcrumb navigation element that provides location context and quick object switching.

Breadcrumb format: `Objects >> [Object Dropdown]`

- 'Objects' is a clickable link that navigates back to the scorecard view.
- The object name is a dropdown pre-selected with the current object, populated with all objects in the user's configured data model.
- Selecting a different object from the dropdown navigates to that object's detail page.
- The breadcrumb is right-aligned with 8px top margin.

Edge case: If a user has two windows open and deletes an object in one, the dropdown in the other window may still list it. The existing validation rule (MBT-25) handles this scenario on navigation.
acceptance_criteria: 'Objects' link navigates back to the scorecard view
Current object name is a dropdown control pre-selected with the active object
Dropdown lists all Salesforce objects in the user's configured data model
Selecting a different object navigates to that object's detail page
Breadcrumb is right-aligned with 8px top margin
ux_changes: Adds a breadcrumb bar at the top of the detail page with a clickable 'Objects' link and a dropdown for switching objects. Right-aligned layout with 8px top margin.
middleware_changes: Loads the list of configured objects for the current user to populate the breadcrumb dropdown on page load.
implementation_notes: The dropdown is populated from the user's data model on page load. When the dropdown value changes, the page reloads with the new object parameter. The validation rule from MBT-25 handles stale object references.
comment_summary: Developer added breadcrumbs with an object dropdown that reloads the page on selection change. Expressed uncertainty about the approach but it was confirmed as correct behavior.
